programme.title: Around the ACL
programme.desc: ATA 216: Rhode Island Preview, Open #7 Sleepers, and Bust or Breakthrough? Open #7 heads to Rhode Island, and Around the ACL is getting you ready for another packed weekend of cornhole. Meesh, Anthony, and Mike Hoffman break down the Singles field, looking at the biggest threats, potential bracket busters, and which players could make serious noise this weekend. Then it’s on to Doubles, where new teams continue trying to break into the top tier while established duos look to protect their spot near the top of the standings. Which partnerships are trending up—and who’s under pressure heading into Rhode Island? To close the show, we play Bust or Breakthrough, predicting whether certain players and teams are about to make a statement… or hit a rough patch. Featuring names like Jimmy Youmans, Jackson Gore, Jack McKinney / Jayce Fleisher, and more. Bracket talk, bold predictions, and a few risky takes—this episode sets the stage for Rhode Island.

programme.desc: In Hell Town (aka Born to the West) John Wayne is back in the saddle as two-fisted Dare Rudd, a roaming cowhand trying to prove he can win a woman's love and outwit a crooked saloon owner. This exciting B-movie from Paramount Pictures was based on a no...

programme.title: Roamin’ Wild (1936
programme.desc: A drifter becomes involved in a dispute between ranchers and outlaws, uncovering a scheme of land grabbing and deception while working to bring the criminals to justice and restore order to the range

programme.title: Range Warfare (1935)
programme.desc: A cowboy returns to his hometown to find it under the control of a ruthless gang, and sets out to expose their crimes and restore order with the help of loyal allies

programme.title: Song of Texas (1943)
programme.desc: A cowboy singer rides into Texas and becomes entangled in a land dispute between honest settlers and a group of outlaws. Using both his quick wits and musical charm, he helps bring justice to the territory while protecting those caught in the conflict.

programme.title: The Squaw Man (1931)
programme.desc: An English aristocrat accused of a crime he did not commit flees to the American West, where he starts a new life and falls in love with a Native American woman. As he builds a future on the frontier, his past returns to threaten the family he has created, forcing him to face difficult choices between honor, love, and redemption.

programme.title: China 9, Liberty 37 (1978)
programme.desc: In China 9, Liberty 37, Fabio Testi stars as a condemned gunfighter offered freedom if he kills an aging rancher (Warren Oates). But when he meets his intended victim—and falls for the rancher’s restless wife (Jenny Agutter)—the deal turns deadly. Torn between passion, loyalty, and survival, the three are swept into a violent showdown where betrayal and desire collide. With raw emotion, striking landscapes, and gritty shootouts, this revisionist Western blurs the line between love and vengeance.

programme.title: Terror at Black Falls (1962)
programme.desc: In Terror at Black Falls, a quiet frontier town is shaken when a bitter land feud erupts into violence. With murder, betrayal, and greed tearing the community apart, one man must stand against the terror sweeping the valley. Gritty, fast-paced, and filled with danger, this hard-hitting Western lives up to its name.

programme.title: Django Meets Santana
programme.desc: The small desert town of Black City is held in a reign of terror by a nasty gang of criminals lead by the ruthless Bud Willer. Earnest, but inexperienced Sheriff Jack Ronson arrives in town to establish law and order. Mysterious bounty hunter Django helps Ronson out.
